response.justification: The candidate is a broad, experienced senior full-stack generalist with genuine strengths in testing culture, open-source leadership, and teaching, but he misses the core minimum qualifications for this role. Professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — far from the required 8 years — and his large-scale infrastructure experience is limited to about two years of high-throughput work at Sociomantic, mostly in DLang rather than C++. He has mentored through workshops and founded a platform, but has not held a formal technical-leadership role driving cross-team strategy for a compute platform.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering-practice foundation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and author of test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing via multi-day courses, workshops, and a decade of open-source project leadership (GISCollective)
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 7 months professionally versus the 8-year minimum; C++ is absent from his current skills list
Large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems experience (~2 years) falls well short of the 5-year requirement, and recent work is product-focused full-stack TypeScript
No formal technical-lead role owning cross-team technical strategy, priorities, and deliverables for a platform organization

key_factors.factor: High-throughput systems and mentoring track record
